text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

INDICATION: 59-year-old male with known metastatic sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma (KRAS G12D-mutant, microsatellite stable), status post laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy, now on FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab as second-line therapy. Restaging examination following completion of 12 cycles. History of prior hepatic metastases (four lesions) and bilateral pulmonary metastases (two nodules identified on prior restaging study). Comparison made to prior CT dated approximately 3 months prior as well as original staging and multiple interim studies on file.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of intravenous iohexol contrast. Images were acquired in the portal venous phase. Axial, coronal, and sagittal reformations reviewed. Oral contrast administered. Radiation dose minimization protocol utilized.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: There are two pulmonary nodules present in the right lung, both of which are unchanged in size when compared to the prior examination. The right lower lobe nodule measures 0.7 cm in greatest axial dimension, essentially identical to the 0.7 cm measurement on the prior study. This lesion demonstrates well-defined borders and has a solid morphology without evidence of cavitation or associated ground-glass opacity. The right upper lobe nodule measures 0.4 cm, representing a slight reduction from the 0.5 cm measured at prior study, now appearing quite small and stable in conspicuity. Both nodules remain morphologically consistent with the clinical history of metastatic disease, though neither has grown in the interval. No new pulmonary nodules are identified in either lung. No pleural effusions bilaterally. No consolidative opacities, ground-glass changes, or interstitial abnormalities. The major airways are patent. No endobronchial lesions.

Pleura and Pericardium: No pleural thickening. No pericardial effusion. Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits for body habitus. The great vessels are unremarkable.

Mediastinum and Hila: No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. The anterior mediastinal fat planes are preserved. No anterior mediastinal mass. Thyroid gland, to the extent visualized, is unremarkable.

Chest Wall and Bony Thorax: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions. Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine without evidence of pathologic fracture or cortical destruction. No rib lesions.

ABDOMEN:

Liver and Biliary System: Four previously identified hepatic lesions are again seen. All four lesions remain stable in size or demonstrate continued slight diminution when compared to the prior examination, consistent with maintained response to ongoing systemic therapy.

The segment VI lesion is a round, hypodense, rim-enhancing focus now measuring 1.3 cm in greatest dimension (previously 1.4 cm on the prior study). This lesion, which at baseline measured 3.1 cm, has shown dramatic interval reduction through successive studies and remains at its nadir or near-nadir on the current examination. Internal architecture appears homogeneous with no worrisome features such as internal hemorrhage or liquefaction suggesting uncontrolled growth.

The segment IVb lesion now measures approximately 1.0 cm (stable, previously 1.0 cm on prior study). This lesion also shows the portal venous phase hypoenhancement characteristic of metastatic deposit, with thin and well-defined margins.

The segment II lesion now measures approximately 0.5 cm, unchanged from the prior restaging study. This lesion is at the lower limit of reliable measurement reproducibility on CT but continues to appear stable in character.

The segment VII lesion is now quite small, measuring approximately 0.3 cm, representing a very subtle hypodense focus which is again seen in its expected location and is slightly smaller or stable compared to the 0.3-0.4 cm range noted on the prior study. This lesion remains barely perceptible but is reproducibly identified.

There is no evidence of new hepatic lesions. The background liver parenchyma appears unremarkable without steatosis or cirrhotic morphology. The intrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated. The main portal vein and hepatic veins are patent without evidence of tumor thrombus. The gallbladder is present and unremarkable. The common bile duct is not dilated.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No splenic lesions.

Pancreas: The pancreatic head, body, and tail are unremarkable. No pancreatic duct dilation. No peripancreatic stranding or mass. Incidentally noted very small (<5 mm) pancreatic cyst in the body -- this has not changed from prior studies and is considered incidental.

Adrenal Glands: Bilateral adrenal glands are visualized without focal nodularity or thickening. No adrenal masses.

Kidneys and Ureters: The right and left kidneys are normal in size and shape with symmetric nephrographic enhancement. A 0.6 cm simple-appearing cyst is again present in the lower pole of the right kidney, stable and unchanged. No hydronephrosis. No renal masses of concern.

Gastrointestinal Tract: The surgical site following prior laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy demonstrates expected post-operative changes -- there is no evidence of anastomotic recurrence. No soft tissue thickening or mass at the anastomotic site. The remaining bowel loops are unremarkable in caliber and wall thickness without obstruction or evidence of ileus. No free intraperitoneal air.

Peritoneum and Mesentery: No ascites. No peritoneal implants. The mesenteric fat planes are preserved without evidence of peritoneal carcinomatosis. No omental thickening or nodularity. The mesenteric and retroperitoneal lymph nodes are not pathologically enlarged.

Vascular Structures: The abdominal aorta is of normal caliber. The celiac, superior mesenteric, and inferior mesenteric arteries are patent to the extent evaluated. No vascular complications identified.

PELVIS:

Pelvic Organs: No pelvic lymphadenopathy. The urinary bladder is well distended and unremarkable in wall thickness. No intraluminal filling defects. The prostate is mildly enlarged in a pattern consistent with benign prostatic hyperplasia -- unchanged from prior studies. The seminal vesicles appear normal.

Bones and Soft Tissues: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions involving the visualized pelvis or proximal femora. Mild degenerative changes of the lumbosacral spine. Pelvic sidewall soft tissues are unremarkable. No free fluid in the pelvis.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Maintained response in all four hepatic metastatic lesions. The segment VI lesion (now 1.3 cm, down from a 3.1 cm baseline) and the segment IVb lesion (1.0 cm, down from 2.2 cm at baseline) continue to demonstrate marked reduction from the pre-treatment appearance with no interval growth on the current study. The segment II and segment VII lesions remain small and stable. There are no new hepatic lesions.

2. Bilateral pulmonary nodules stable in size and character. The right lower lobe nodule (0.7 cm) and right upper lobe nodule (0.4 cm) are unchanged from the prior examination. No new pulmonary lesions identified.

3. No new sites of metastatic disease identified in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis. No peritoneal implants, no pathologic lymphadenopathy, no bone lesions.

4. Intact post-surgical anatomy at the sigmoid colectomy site without locoregional recurrence.

5. Stable minor incidental findings as above (simple renal cyst, small pancreatic body cyst, benign prostatic hypertrophy).

Overall, this examination demonstrates sustained disease control with the hepatic metastases remaining substantially smaller than at baseline and no evidence of new metastatic deposits. The pulmonary nodules identified on prior imaging remain stable without progression. These findings are consistent with ongoing favorable response to current systemic therapy.
